texto: Jerzy,” said I, turning to the Dean of Students, who was nearby, “You’re experienced in these things, and I’m not. Will you tell me whether it is an unalterable rule in this school for a teacher to lose himself in the middle of the night scrambling to find what he cannot see and what is probably not there?”“Isn’t that what we do in the classroom every day?” said Witskoc. “Yes, I’m afraid that’s how it is. I’d like to see how you can construct a pedagogical context made up of nothing but security and certainties.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eric Mace-Tessler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Jerzy,” said I, turning to the Dean of Students, who was nearby, “You’re experienced in these thi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, humor, teaching pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Jerzy,” said I, turning to the Dean of Students, who was nearby, “You’re experienced in these things, and I’m not. Will you tell me whether it is an unalterable rule in this school for a teacher to lose himself in the middle of the night scrambling to find what he cannot see and what is probably not there?”“Isn’t that what we do in the classroom every day?” said Witskoc. “Yes, I’m afraid that’s how it is. I’d like to see how you can construct a pedagogical context made up of nothing but security and certainties.
